disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Classic 'first-step' morning pain that eases with activity and worsens after prolonged standing; point tenderness at the medial calcaneal tubercle; pain on toe dorsiflexion (windlass) — plantar fasciitis pattern
trigger_question_examples: Is it worst on your very first steps in the morning and eases as you get going, then worse after standing a while?
Where exactly is the tender spot — the underside/front-inner part of the heel?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — worst on the first steps, eases as I walk, worse after standing; the tender spot is the front-inner underside of the heel.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No night/rest pain, no diffuse calcaneal 'squeeze' bony pain, no history of a sudden 'pop'/inability to push off (screens stress fracture / Achilles rupture)
trigger_question_examples: Do you get heel pain at night or at rest, or does squeezing the sides of the heel bone hurt?
Did you feel a sudden pop, and can you still push off / rise onto your toes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No night or rest pain, squeezing the sides of the heel doesn't hurt, no pop, and I can rise onto my toes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No burning/tingling radiating into the sole/toes, no numbness (screens tarsal tunnel/neuropathic causes)
trigger_question_examples: Any burning, tingling or numbness spreading into the sole or toes?
Is it a sharp mechanical pain rather than a nerve-type burning?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No burning or tingling into the toes — it's a sharp mechanical stab.
